summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn addressed those feeling weary, oppressed, and under intense spiritual attack. He acknowledged the reality of a strategic, demonic war against believers' lives, health, families, and minds, but his core message was a call to fight back with the divine tools God provides. The sermon focused on the first two pieces of the Armor of God from Ephesians 6. First, the Belt of Truth emphasizes building one's life on God's Word and rejecting the devil's lies and deception, which often begin by questioning God's commands. Second, the Breastplate of Righteousness protects the heart, satisfies the soul, and brings God's favor; it is not about perfection but about surrendering to Christ's righteousness and pursuing a life aligned with God. Pastor Travis concluded with an invitation for listeners to surrender to Jesus, step into the light, and live for God.
mainPoints: Believers are in a real spiritual war and must be alert and prepared to fight back, not with worldly weapons but with divine power.
The first piece of spiritual armor is the Belt of Truth, which requires centering one's life on God's Word to combat the devil's lies and deception.
The second piece is the Breastplate of Righteousness, which protects the heart, satisfies the soul, and is obtained through surrender to Christ, not self-effort.

versesMentioned.text: Exercise daily in God—no spiritual flabbiness, please! Workouts in the gymnasium are useful, but a disciplined life in God is far more so, making you fit both today and forever.

Finally, my brethren, be strong in the Lord and in the power of His might. Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil. For we do not wrestle against flesh and blood, but against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the darkness of this age, against spiritual hosts of wickedness in the heavenly places. Therefore take up the whole armor of God, that you may be able to withstand in the evil day, and having done all, to stand. Stand therefore, having girded your waist with truth, having put on the breastplate of righteousness, and having shod your feet with the preparation of the gospel of peace; above all, taking the shield of faith with which you will be able to quench all the fiery darts of the wicked one. And take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God; praying always with all prayer and supplication in the Spirit, being watchful to this end with all perseverance and supplication for all the saints.
Be alert and of sober mind. Your enemy the devil prowls around like a roaring lion looking for someone to devour.
For though we live in the world, we do not wage war as the world does. The weapons we fight with are not the weapons of the world. On the contrary, they have divine power to demolish strongholds.
Jesus answered, "I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me."
Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free."

You belong to your father, the devil, and you want to carry out your father’s desires. He was a murderer from the beginning, not holding to the truth, for there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks his native language, for he is a liar and the father of lies.
And no wonder, for Satan himself masquerades as an angel of light.
But I am afraid that just as Eve was deceived by the serpent’s cunning, your minds may somehow be led astray from your sincere and pure devotion to Christ.

Now the serpent was more crafty than any of the wild animals the Lord God had made. He said to the woman, "Did God really say, 'You must not eat from any tree in the garden'?" The woman said to the serpent, "We may eat fruit from the trees in the garden, but God did say, 'You must not eat fruit from the tree that is in the middle of the garden, and you must not touch it, or you will die.'" "You will not certainly die," the serpent said to the woman. "For God knows that when you eat from it your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God, knowing good and evil." When the woman saw that the fruit of the tree was good for food and pleasing to the eye, and also desirable for gaining wisdom, she took some and ate it. She also gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he ate it. Then the eyes of both of them were opened, and they realized they were naked; so they sewed fig leaves together and made coverings for themselves.
Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.
The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.
Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled.
But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
But you, man of God, flee from all this, and pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, endurance and gentleness.
Flee the evil desires of youth and pursue righteousness, faith, love and peace, along with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart.
Surely, Lord, you bless the righteous; you surround them with your favor as with a shield.
Therefore confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective.
All of us have become like one who is unclean, and all our righteous acts are like filthy rags; we all shrivel up like a leaf, and like the wind our sins sweep us away.

Monday.scripture: Ephesians 6:10-11 (NIV) - "Finally, be strong in the Lord and in his mighty power. Put on the full armor of God, so that you can take your stand against the devil’s schemes."
Monday.action: Identify one area of your life where you feel under attack or weary. Acknowledge it as a spiritual battle, not just a circumstance.
Monday.prayer: Lord, open my eyes to the reality of the spiritual war. Help me to be strong in You and to put on Your armor today.

Tuesday.scripture: 1 Timothy 4:7-8 (MSG) - "Exercise daily in God—no spiritual flabbiness, please!"
Tuesday.action: Commit to a 'bite-sized' spiritual workout: read one chapter of the Bible and pray for 5 minutes.
Tuesday.prayer: God, I surrender my lack of discipline. Help me to exercise daily in You, starting with small, faithful steps.

Wednesday.scripture: John 8:32 (NIV) - "Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free."
Wednesday.action: Write down one lie you've believed about yourself, your situation, or God. Next to it, write a biblical truth that counters it.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, You are the Truth. Expose the lies I've believed and anchor my heart in Your liberating truth today.

Thursday.scripture: Proverbs 4:23 (NIV) - "Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it."
Thursday.action: Evaluate one area where you need to guard your heart (e.g., media intake, a relationship, your thoughts). Take one practical step to protect it.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, protect my heart. Clothe me in Christ's righteousness and guard me from anything that would steal my purity or purpose.

Friday.scripture: Matthew 5:6 (NIV) - "Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled."
Friday.action: Instead of asking God for a blessing or breakthrough today, pray, "God, make me hungry and thirsty for Your righteousness."
Friday.prayer: Father, shift my desires. Let my deepest hunger be for right living with You, trusting that You will satisfy my soul.

Saturday.scripture: James 5:16 (NIV) - "The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective."
Saturday.action: Spend time in prayer, confessing any known sin to God. Then, pray specifically for a need in your life or someone else's, trusting in the power of righteous prayer.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, I confess my sins and receive Your forgiveness. I stand in Christ's righteousness and pray with faith, believing Your power is at work.
